summary: Toponym register from Incahuasi — the most lexically concentrated khipu in the corpus. 30 cords, 12 STRING ALL qaqa (rock), 100% hits. A compact index of 12 named rock landmarks in 3 clusters. Possibly a surveyor's pocket reference listing boundary rocks for a specific zone.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  30 cords. 12 STRING (40%, 100% hits, ALL qaqa), 9 INT, 8 EMPTY, 1 L-1.
  Architecture: 0-SEUL, depth L1. 3 clusters. Colors: AB(20), MB(8), MG(2).
  Provenance: Incahuasi. Museum: Proyecto Incahuasi.

VOCABULARY
  qaqa x12. ONE word, TWELVE times. Most lexically concentrated khipu in the corpus.

NARRATIVE
  Rock. Twelve times, and nothing else. Each *qaqa* names a stone landmark at Incahuasi. Three clusters divide the twelve into groups — boundaries of three zones, or waypoints of three routes. Nine numbers annotate the rocks. Thirty cords, one word, twelve rocks. A surveyor's pocket reference.
